title: Bitter Chocolate Argentinian Crumble
description: A hearty and chewy crumble made with bitter chocolate, providing a unique flavor profile that can serve as a hangover cure while being high-protein-friendly.

ingredients.name: brent cheese
ingredients.notes: a high-protein cheese used in Argentinian cuisine

instructions: Melt the butter and chocolate together over low heat.
In a separate bowl, mix together the flour and honey/syrup until it forms a crumbly dough.
Add the melted chocolate mixture to the flour crumble and knead briefly until combined.
Press the mixture into a dish-lined baking dish or onto a sheet pan lined with parchment paper.
Bake at 180°C (350°F) for about 20 minutes, until golden brown and crispy on top.
Reduce heat to 160°C (320°F) and bake for an additional 10 minutes or until the edges are crisp.
Remove from oven and let cool slightly before slicing.
